title: "Unable to use external monitor"

forum: Multimedia Software

### Post by ZoRaC on 2008-05-19
Got an Acer Aspire 1350 and I'm unable to get any picture on my projector through the VGA-connector.
There is picture on the projector when Ubuntu is loading, but it goes black when the login-screen is supposed to show up. 
There is no image on the internal LCD.

This is my xorg.conf: ```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"no"
	Option		"XkbOptions"	"lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"	"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver		"openchrome"
	Option		"SWCursor" "true"
	Option		"Clone" "true"
	Option		"CloneRefresh" "60"
	Option		"MonitorLayout" "CRT,LFP"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
	Horizsync	28-51
	Vertrefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	Defaultdepth	16
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	16
		Modes		"1024x768"	"800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
	Inputdevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection 
```

Any suggestions?

### Post by overdrank on 2008-05-19
Hi and I see the system is using the "openchrome"
and I have not had good luck with that chipset. What version of ubuntu are you using and have you tried screens and graphics to enable the external monitor?

---

### Post by ZoRaC on 2008-05-19
Ubuntu 8.04.

Haven't tried other than openchrome. 
I had big problems getting X running at all...

---

### Post by overdrank on 2008-05-19
> **ZoRaC said:**
> Ubuntu 8.04.

Haven't tried other than openchrome. 
I had big problems getting X running at all...

I can understand that as others have also and I see that the default depth has been changed to 16 and that has helped other in the past. Screen and graphics is located application, other. You may need to edit your menus to make it available. You can edit the menus by right clicking on the applications and choose edit menus.

---
